body:
**Breaking:** Removed `onClearErrors` prop from `<Form>`

Demo: https://deploy-preview-3136--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/form#submit-with-a-server-function

For all validation modes, it's a better UX to optimistically clear external (server) errors on change without needing to add `onClearErrors={setErrors}`
- Matches [RA](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/forms.html#server-validation:~:text=These%20are%20displayed%20to%20the%20user%20as%20soon%20as%20the%20validationErrors%20prop%20is%20set%2C%20and%20cleared%20after%20the%20user%20modifies%20each%20field%27s%20value)
- Makes `useActionState` viable, compare with [the preview](https://deploy-preview-2983--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/form#submit-with-a-server-function) of this [PR](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/2983), it would always require an extra state and effect 

Also frees up the API surface a bit if there's a need to customize revalidation more deeply in the future

Closes https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/2758

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).
title: [form] Drop `onClearErrors` prop and always clear `errors` on change
